select_when: Standing still effectively means losing ground because rivals, substitutes, or environmental pressures are improving at the same time.
A team must distinguish maintenance effort from real advantage and decide where continuous adaptation is non-optional.
Competitive moves, regulation, or technology shifts are changing the baseline faster than annual planning or static playbooks can absorb.
The work needs an anti-complacency frame that links ongoing adaptation to feedback rather than to one-off heroics.
avoid_when: The pressure to keep running is pushing the team into analysis paralysis, feature creep, or action-first thrash without clear problem definition.
The model is being used to excuse speed without naming the safety thresholds, execution limits, or cognitive load that keep adaptation sane.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on relative adaptation pressure, anti-complacency, and feedback-guided response rather than widening it into generic hustle rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor competition sprawl beyond the raw source's clearer anti-complacency, feedback-loop, and execution-discipline surface.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans game theory, scenario analysis, and continuous improvement discipline.

select_when.note: The source is explicit that the model is strongest when standing still means losing ground and when adaptation pressure outpaces static planning.

avoid_when.note: The risk section directly warns about analysis paralysis, complexity creep, and action outrunning diagnosis.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on competitive adaptation pressure, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields anti-complacency strategy and adaptation framing, but the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model is naturally diagnostic of adaptation pressure and systemic in how moving baselines shape outcomes, while the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
